subject: Sociology
title: Ethnography and Observation
description: A short lesson to help you apply Ethnography and Observation.

hook.text: To understand a gang, a sociologist moved into their neighborhood for three years. Surveys wouldn't have cut it.

buildup.text: Ethnography means immersing yourself in a community, observing daily life, and recording what you see firsthand.

discovery.text: Participant observation reveals what people actually do, not just what they say they do — those often differ.

twist.text: Going native is a risk. The longer you embed, the harder it becomes to stay objective about the group.

climax.text: Ethnography trades breadth for depth. You can't generalize easily, but you capture meaning surveys never reach.

punchline.text: To understand a world, sometimes you have to live in it.

quiz.question: What is a key advantage of ethnography over surveys?
options: It reveals what people actually do, not just what they say
It requires less time than other methods
